LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF GUY KORNBLUM'S APPLICATION FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HIS INITIAL BURDEN OF PROOF. (CCP 484.090.) ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF HAS STATED FACTS TO SUPPORT HIS APPLICATION (APPLICATION, 7C), THERE IS NO DECLARATION SHOWING PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE OR ESTABLISHING FOUNDATION FOR THE PROFFERED FACTS. "EVEN IF YOU CHECK THE THIRD BOX, HOWEVER, YOU MUST STILL SUPPORT YOUR FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS BY WAY OF DECLARATIONS OR AFFIDAVITS." (SEE WEIL & BROWN, CAL. PRACTICE GUIDE: CIV. PROC. Plaintiff Guy Kornblum'S Application For Right To Attach Order; Notice
Off Calendar Mar-19-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF GUY KORNBLUM'S Application For Right To Attach Order is denied. Plaintiff has not met his initial burden of proof. (CCP 484.090.) Although Plaintiff has stated facts to support his application (Application, 7c), there is no declaration showing personal knowledge or establishing foundation for the proffered facts. "Even if you check the third box, however, you must still support your factual allegations by way of declarations or affidavits." (See Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial (Rutter) 9:906.1.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
